text.title: Launch-Day Warroom Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every launch-day war room must enforce. These prevent the most common failure: coordination breakdown when traffic spikes.

rule.statement: Every role (launch director, content captain, ops lead, support triage, exec stand-in) is named with primary + named backup. Reject launches with any role unfilled OR backup missing OR a single person holding two roles.
rule.rationale: Empty or doubled roles produce the "everyone thought someone else was watching" failure. Backups handle the inevitable absence (sick day, network outage, parallel duty).

rule.statement: Run a 60-minute dress rehearsal 48-72 hours before launch. Walk through hourly checkpoints, post test messages to launch channels, simulate one rollback scenario. Reject launches without a recorded dress rehearsal.
rule.rationale: Rehearsals expose template errors and channel-permission bugs at near-zero cost. Teams that rehearse have 3-5× lower MTTR during launch incidents.

rule.statement: Launch day runs on hourly checkpoints, each with explicit decisions: traffic_status, channel_post_status, support_queue_depth, error_rate, next_hour_action. Reject silent hours: every hour produces a logged update.
rule.rationale: Hourly cadence prevents drift and surfaces issues 6× faster than ad-hoc updates. Silence is the loudest failure signal.

rule.statement: Every decision class (post tweet, halt email send, pause PH push, scale infra) has a named decider AND threshold. Reject ambiguous decision authority. Decisions over launch-stop authority belong to launch director with exec stand-in tiebreaker.
rule.rationale: Unambiguous decision rights cut deliberation latency by 50%+ during high-pressure windows. RACI matrices apply during launches too.

rule.statement: A rollback playbook covers: partial rollback (retract one channel), full rollback (pull PH listing + email retraction + status page update), exec-comms template. Rollback decision criteria documented (e.g., "error rate > 5% sustained 30 min triggers partial"). Reject launches without rollback playbook.
rule.rationale: Launches that go sideways without rollback playbooks compound damage. Pre-defined rollback turns chaos into a known procedure.

example.title: War-room runbook with named roles
example.code:
Launch: AcmeNext v2 — Product Hunt + email + Twitter Window: 2026-05-22 07:00 PT - 19:00 PT
Roles: Launch director: @jdoe (backup: @asmith) Content captain: @kchen (backup: @rgomez) Ops lead: @tparker (backup: @sosborne) Support triage: @ljohnson (backup: @nyamada) Exec stand-in: @mceo
Channels: #launch-control — decisions, hourly updates (all leads + exec) #launch-content — copy review, asset publishing (content + director) #launch-support — incoming issues, customer responses (support) #launch-exec — exec-only briefings on issues > sev3
example.note: Illustrates r1 + r3 + r4 in a launch-day setup.

example.title: Chaos by Slack thread
example.code: DM thread of 8 people. No named roles. PM is asking on Slack "who's posting to PH at 7am?" Marketing lead asks "should we hold the email send if signups slow?" — no answer. Engineer pushes hotfix without notifying support, who get blindsided by panicked customers.
example.note: Illustrates r1, r3, r4 violations. Common shape of launches that fail on coordination.
